summary: 2026-06-10 21:39:53 PDT — I found two blocker-level gaps against the security-sensitive acceptance criteria: app-context validation can fail open for audience/expiration, and `/api/rates` accepts arbitrary top-level objects instead of validating the rate config shape and numeric values. AC8 remains post-merge/live-volume only, as noted in the spec.

findings.title: App context validation fails open for aud and exp
findings.claim: `resolveUid` only checks `aud` when `clientId` is truthy, and only rejects expiration when `exp` is already a number. Because `requirePresenter` does not require `ZOOM_CLIENT_ID`, a deployment with `RATE_STORE_KEY` and `ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET` but no client id will accept a decryptable context without verifying `aud`; a payload with missing or non-numeric `exp` is also accepted instead of failing closed. This violates AC1's required `aud`/`iss`/`exp` validation for the identity used to key private rate data.
findings.suggestion: Treat missing `ZOOM_CLIENT_ID` as rate-store unconfigured, require `p.aud === clientId`, and reject unless `Number.isFinite(p.exp)` and `now / 1000 <= p.exp`. Add tests for missing client id, missing exp, and non-numeric exp.

findings.title: PUT /api/rates does not validate config schema or numeric rates
findings.claim: The endpoint passes `req.body` directly to `rateStore.save`, whose guard only checks that the body is a non-array object. As a result, malformed configs such as `rateTable: "oops"`, non-finite/string rates, or invalid numeric settings are persisted with 200, contrary to AC4's requirement that the endpoint validate body shape and rate values before saving.
findings.suggestion: Add a server-side validator before saving: require expected array/object fields, validate strings/ids, coerce or reject numeric fields consistently, reject non-finite/negative rates, and cover invalid bodies in endpoint tests.
